old_claim.note: Basic Fermentation is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No direct fermentation dependency remains because the node's source-backed anchor is ochre processing and material transformation, not food or beverage fermentation.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract: the Science paper reports a 100,000-year-old ochre-processing workshop with grinding, scraping, mixing, and storage of pigment-rich compound.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports pigment and compound processing as material transformation, not fermentation as a direct predecessor.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Fermentation is another transformation technology, but the retained pigment_creation edge is the direct source-backed material-processing lineage for this broad node.

ontology_before: Early Alchemy & Material Science depended on Basic Fermentation as an unsourced historical predecessor.
ontology_after: Early Alchemy & Material Science keeps Pigment Creation as the direct source-backed material-transformation prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: fermentation_basic is no longer a direct prerequisite for early_alchemy_material_science.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to biochemical fermentation chemistry.
A stronger source showed fermentation directly led to the material-processing practices represented here.
A separate early_biochemical_transformation node were added and intentionally tied to fermentation.
short_reason: Fermentation is parallel transformation context, not the source-backed material anchor.
